question: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
gr_label: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
help_text:
A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

question: Are you a Marketplace Facilitator? Marketplace Facilitators provide a forum, shop, and/or website where businesses sell TPP or services (think Amazon, Etsy, etc.). Marketplace sellers who sell on these platforms are NOT marketplace facilitators.
help_text:
Marketplace Facilitators are a marketplace that contracts with third party sellers to promote their sales of goods or services. Some states have different filing requirements for Marketplace Facilitators for remitting the tax collected for all their third-party sellers.

Examples of Marketplace Facilitators are Etsy, Amazon, Ebay, Wayfair, etc.
Please be aware that by clicking this boolean, it may mean we have to file an extra schedule to support your registration type. Avalara will not be responsible for incorrect or missing selection of this check box.

question: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
gr_label: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
help_text:
A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

question: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
gr_label: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
help_text:
A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

question: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
gr_label: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
help_text:
A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

gr_label: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
help_text:
A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

gr_label: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
help_text:
A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

question: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
gr_label: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
help_text:
A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

question: Indiana State Registration ID
help_text:
Find your registration ID by logging into your Indiana e-filing account and checking your profile, or by checking the documents you received when you registered your business to collect and remit sales tax in Indiana.
Example registration ID: 1234567890-001
The three digits after the account ID are your location ID. Please ensure this is correct. You may need to set up multiple calendars for different locations.

A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

question: New York Sales Tax Identification Number
help_text:
Your state registration ID is most likely the same as your Federal Employee ID Number (FEIN). You're assigned a New York Sales Tax ID Number only if you don't already have a FEIN.

Find your temporary New York ID number in the documents you received when you registered your business to collect and remit sales and use tax in New York. This number will be used to identify your returns in New York until you get a FEIN.
Your ID will be 9 digits OR will begin with TF or NY + 7 digits OR 9 digits + suffix C, SC, SS, S1, S2 or any number ranging from 01-99. Examples: 123456789, TF1234567, 123456789SC or 12345678922

question: New York Promptax Access code
help_text:
Please provide your 6 digit New York Promptax Access code.

To locate this code, do the following:
1) Login to your New York online services account.
2) From the left-menu panel, select Promptax sales and use tax.
3) Under Enrollment Information, locate the 6-digit access code.

Please note that if this number is not correct Avalara’s payment on your behalf may not apply correctly and Avalara is not responsible for any penalty and interest due to incorrect Access Codes.

question: New York Sales Tax Identification Number
help_text:
Your state registration ID is most likely the same as your Federal Employee ID Number (FEIN). You're assigned a New York Sales Tax ID Number only if you don't already have a FEIN.

Find your temporary New York ID number in the documents you received when you registered your business to collect and remit sales and use tax in New York. This number will be used to identify your returns in New York until you get a FEIN.
Your ID will be 9 digits OR will begin with TF or NY + 7 digits OR 9 digits + suffix C, SC, SS, S1, S2 or any number ranging from 01-99. Examples: 123456789, TF1234567, 123456789SC or 12345678922

question: New York Promptax Access code
help_text:
Please provide your 6 digit New York Promptax Access code.

To locate this code, do the following:
1) Login to your New York online services account.
2) From the left-menu panel, select Promptax sales and use tax.
3) Under Enrollment Information, locate the 6-digit access code.

Please note that if this number is not correct Avalara’s payment on your behalf may not apply correctly and Avalara is not responsible for any penalty and interest due to incorrect Access Codes.

question: Ohio UST 1 Vendor's License Number
help_text:
Find your vendor's license number by checking the documentation you received when you registered your business for a vendor’s license in Ohio. A taxpayer cannot file a sales or use tax return using their Social Security number or FEIN. Vendor’s license numbers that have a prefix of 95 or 96 are not sales or use tax accounts.

If your vendor's license number is one of the following, you should file the UST 1 return for sales tax:
County vendor’s licenses – prefix 01 thru 88
Master cumulative accounts – prefix 89 or 93
Transient vendor’s licenses – prefix 89, 90, 91, 94, or 99

Example vendor's license number: 99-123456

help_text:
A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

question: Tennessee Account Number
help_text:
Find your account number by checking the documents you received when you registered your business to collect and remit sales and use tax in Tennessee.

Example account numbers: 0123456789-SLC, 0123456789-STV, 0123456789-LBD or 0123456789-BUS

If you have a sales tax account, your account number should end with the letters SLC. If you have a TV & Telecom account, your account number should end with the letters STV. If you have a Liquor By The Drink Tax account, your account number should end with the letters LBD. If you have a Business Tax account, your account number should end with the letters BUS.

A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

Find your Washington UBI by checking the documents you received when you registered your business to collect and remit sales and use tax in Washington.
Example UBI: 602-123-456
Washington's MyDOR website will occasionally require us to update an existing password upon login. If prompted, Avalara may update your password in order to continue filing on your behalf. If you are unable to login to your account, please make sure that you check this page for updated login credentials before contacting Avalara Support.

question: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
gr_label: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)
help_text:
A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789

question: West Virginia Account Number
help_text:
Find your account number by checking the documents you received when you registered your business to collect and remit sales and use tax in West Virginia.

Example account number: 2341-5678 or 23415678

Please note West Virginia assigns different account numbers for different types. These account numbers usually only have a one-digit difference. Please contact West Virginia to confirm the account number for the Sales and Use tax account.
